claims.evidence_text: The average winter wheat yields were 18% lower under no‐till and 6% lower under reduced tillage than underconventionalmoldboardplowingafter17years(from2001to2018).
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat yields were 18% lower under no-tillage and 6% lower under reduced (rotary) tillage than under conventional moldboard plowing over 17 years in the North China Plain.
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, irrigated, North China Plain

claims.evidence_text: Wheat straw was chopped and spread on the soil in all treatments and maize seeded without prior tillage. ... CT was the most robust in terms of crop yields and soil C sequestration.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat straw chopped and spread on the soil surface in all tillage treatments; conventional tillage with maize straw return gave the highest wheat yields and soil C sequestration.
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, North China Plain

claims.evidence_text: The experimenthadacommonandcontinuousdoublecroppingsystemrotationofwinterwheat (Triticum aestivum L.) and summer maize (Zea mays L.). Each year in early June, the winter wheat is harvested and maize is planted; while in early October maize is harvested and winter wheat is plant
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat grown in a continuous winter wheat-summer maize double cropping rotation; wheat harvested early June and sown early October in the North China Plain.
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, irrigated, North China Plain

claims.evidence_text: Farmers ploughed the top25cmofsoilwithamoldboardplough,priortoseeding winterwheatinrowswitharow distance of 12.5 cm.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat seeded in rows with a row distance of 12.5 cm (Hoeksche Waard, Netherlands).

claims.evidence_text: They aim at 250‐300 wheat plants per m2 , 550 to 600 ears per m2 , and a grain yield of ≥10 Mg ha‐1 yr‐1 .
claims.claim_summary: Farmers target 250-300 winter wheat plants per m2.

claims.evidence_text: They aim at 250‐300 wheat plants per m2 , 550 to 600 ears per m2 , and a grain yield of ≥10 Mg ha‐1 yr‐1 .
claims.claim_summary: Farmers target 550-600 wheat ears (spikes) per m2.

claims.evidence_text: Before winter wheatsowing,thefertilizersweredisseminated atarateof130kgha−1 ofNand121kgha−1 of P.
claims.claim_summary: Before winter wheat sowing, fertilizers applied at 130 kg N/ha (plus additional topdress N after jointing).
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, irrigated, North China Plain

claims.evidence_text: Before winter wheatsowing,thefertilizersweredisseminated atarateof130kgha−1 ofNand121kgha−1 of P.
claims.claim_summary: Phosphorus applied at 121 kg P/ha before winter wheat sowing.
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, irrigated, North China Plain

claims.evidence_text: both wheat and maize received 138 kg ha−1 of N as urea, shortlyafterjointingthroughtopdressing.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat received basal N before sowing plus 138 kg N/ha as urea topdressed shortly after jointing.
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, irrigated, North China Plain

claims.evidence_text: Irrigation was applied when the soil moisture in the root zone declined to 60–65% of the field capacity.
claims.claim_summary: Irrigation applied to wheat when root-zone soil moisture declined to 60-65% of field capacity.
claims.management_system: winter wheat-summer maize double cropping, irrigated, North China Plain
